page: Google Cloud Platform h4d-standard-192 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the h4d server family. It features 192 dedicated virtual central processing units and 720.0 gigabytes of system memory. This configuration provides a resource density of 3.75 gigabytes of RAM per virtual core.

This server type does not include local storage or integrated graphics processing units, meaning all storage requirements must be met through external network-attached storage options. Additionally, the instance does not bundle any complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

With its high core count and balanced memory-to-core ratio, the h4d-standard-192 is designed for large-scale general-purpose workloads. It is suitable for multi-threaded applications, enterprise application hosting, and distributed processing tasks that require significant parallel compute capacity without the need for specialized GPU acceleration or local NVMe storage.
description: Google Cloud Platform h4d-standard-192 is an x86_64 architecture server instance within the h4d family, configured with 192 dedicated vCPUs and 720.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a resource density of 3.75 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. Lacking complimentary public IPv4 addresses, it is designed for secure, internal-facing, or load-balanced deployments. The high core count makes it suitable for highly parallelized general-purpose workloads, enterprise applications, and distributed processing tasks that demand substantial compute capacity without specialized hardware acceleration.
